inline_comments.message: is it a scraping config for prometheus? if yes that's cool!
don't we need a password  and/or a more "interesting" name (admin for user admin, user for normal user for example)?
a reason to be in root group?
I'll change that in order to use service templates but __after__ this merge

don't put .svc.cluster.local because cluster.local is installation dependent.
Finishing at the namespace is sufficient.

This is the standard JAAS file and this is used to create a secret. User admin is used to bypass the AAF authentication when the Kafka client is Message Router service. No additional users are required. The user admin was used from the 1st release. The same comments are applicable for remaining JAAS configuration files
We used the the Confluent docker images and we extended the confluent images to add ONAP specific functionality. It is a requirement from the parent docker image
Agree. But it is need to match the Kafka  advertised listener (statefulset with headeless service)
I was complaining on having the password hardcoded here
the trailing space is still here
we have a secret template which should be used but I think in more fine grained way
OK, it was just out of curiosity

nslookup onap-message-router-zookeeper-0.message-router-zookeeper.onap
Server:		10.233.0.3
Address:	10.233.0.3#53

Name:	onap-message-router-zookeeper-0.message-router-zookeeper.onap.svc.cluster.local
Address: 10.233.73.185

so {{ include "common.release" . }}-{{.Values.zookeeper.name}}-0.{{.Values.zookeeper.name}}.{{.Release.Namespace}} should be sufficient no?
the trailing space is still here
the trailing space is still here
Do you  want o read it from values.yaml
AAF was used for auth/authz the users. admin was used to skip the AAF auth for the requests from the Message Router

Looks like the common.secrets template shared is used to create the secret for the env variable. 

is common.secrete template also supports creation of secret for a binary file. if Yes. Can you share a example

you mean that we need to give the exact same name than the one zk has as hostname?
it's not really cloud ready but I do get the point.
Let's keep it this way for now and see later how we can fix it (it would prevent onap to work in some "unusual" installation)
I'll create a JIRA ticket for that.

nope, we don't want that

The problem with a whole file put as a secret (especially with the hardcoded values like you have now) is that those passwords are:
1) Not generated per deployment
2) cannot be easily change with the override file

I'd strongly recommend using the construct with init container and common secret template (even if you'll hardcode the password for now in values.yaml)
same here with the same rationale
replaced with a configmap and sensitive data is replaced in init container
set medium to Memory so that we are sure that passwords are not stored in plain text on a disk
